142622.explanation: We find a good representation of women and men in the executive board important; the quality of the board members is paramount.

142623.explanation: A referendum can be useful if it is properly integrated into the decision-making process.

142627.explanation: P21 is in favor of sustainable energy. The municipality should promote this as well, for example by providing good information or supporting resident initiatives.

142628.explanation: We must limit the fragmentation of the Netherlands and space is scarce. P21 does allow local companies space for a new location to enable housing development.

142630.explanation: In our opinion, employers are responsible for this.

142632.explanation: Wood burning is one of the main causes of air pollution in our municipality. If actively informing residents about the harmful effects of wood burning is insufficient to reduce nuisance, a ban should be considered.

142633.explanation: A blanket, heated cushion, or simply keeping your coat on is more climate-friendly and prevents energy waste.

142634.explanation: After the national ban on fireworks is introduced, we should see if there is room for local initiatives, provided nuisance for animals and people is limited. Organization is not a task for the municipality.

142636.explanation: Even with less noise, festivals and parties can contribute pleasantly to social cohesion, but with less unnecessary nuisance for people and animals.

142639.explanation: Such a scheme already exists and people choose what to spend the budget on, for example sports or culture (reimbursement fund). P21 supports as simple as possible schemes for low-income people, with room for personal control. Therefore, a better increased general allowance.

142640.explanation: Independent local media are important for providing good information and a healthy democracy.

142646.explanation: If the situation arises that more enforcement is (temporarily) needed, we would be willing to allocate funds for it. P21 prefers to invest in prevention.

142647.explanation: Due to privacy intrusion, camera surveillance should, according to P21, only be used in extreme cases. We currently do not see such 'extreme cases' in the municipality of Bunnik.

142649.explanation: It can enable first-time buyers to purchase a home. P21 prefers building more affordable housing. In the long term, starter loans are not a solution because they can have a price-inflating effect.

142652.explanation: P21 believes that people have the right to a home built for permanent residence. Therefore, we advocate for more affordable housing in the municipality.

142653.explanation: The green countryside is important for our villages and the whole region. Therefore, we build as little as possible in this area. Besides the existing plans for Kersenweide and the A12 business park, only limited housing will be developed on the edges of the villages.

142654.explanation: P21 supports that people on social assistance are guided to paid work as quickly and well as possible. Volunteer work can help, provided it suits the person.

142655.explanation: P21 prefers that separate schemes are included in a simple scheme with an increased general allowance that provides sufficient space, so that debt forgiveness is not necessary. Until then, we do believe debt forgiveness is needed in certain situations.

142656.explanation: People in financial hardship must be able to count on sufficient support to participate in our society.

142658.explanation: P21 wants the municipality to actively continue to promote the switch to electric cars. This means continuing to designate charging points where needed.

142665.explanation: The municipal council has already decided that a new business park will be created south of the A12, primarily for local companies to make more housing possible in the villages. Only once this park exists will we consider new plans. Our scarce space requires clear choices.

142667.explanation: For people with urgency, there must be housing. Refugees often lack a safety net in our country unlike many Dutch housing seekers. The rules must ensure people do not have to sleep on the street or keep occupying places in overcrowded reception centers.

142671.explanation: To protect the health of, especially young people, we are against establishing a coffee shop in our municipality.

142672.explanation: Thanks in part to P21, 30% social rental housing in new construction projects has been established in the municipality's current housing policy. We want to continue this policy.

142673.explanation: P21 wants more affordable housing. Higher construction makes it cheaper. Close to amenities and public transport, this reduces road pressure. Therefore, P21 supports a single 9-story apartment building in the Bunnik station area and a 7-story building at Entree Zuid Odijk, well integrated into the surroundings. Not everywhere.

142676.explanation: There appears to be no general problem with parking pressure in our municipality. For village centers, P21 supports more space for cyclists and pedestrians: green village centers with room for people to meet each other.

142679.explanation: The interests of our residents come first. In 2024, Bunnik conducted a governance capacity study: the municipality now functions well as an independent municipality, with regional cooperation.

142622.explanation: The CDA believes the executive board should preferably consist of an equal number of men and women.

142623.explanation: The CDA is against referenda because they increase societal divisions and reduce complex decisions to a simple 'yes' or 'no.' We do support involving residents through participation to gather as much input from society as possible.

142627.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality should stimulate sustainable energy generation, but unfortunately, our municipality currently lacks the financial resources to support this.

142628.explanation: The CDA supports a new business park on the south side of the A12 to retain businesses and small entrepreneurs in our municipality. Within this principle, there must also be space for a distribution center.

142630.explanation: The municipality must allow housing for seasonal workers on the farmyard, provided it meets minimum standards for quality and livability. The employer also has responsibility, but we are not in favor of mandatory on-site housing.

142632.explanation: The CDA wants more attention and measures to improve air quality, using WHO standards as a starting point. A ban on open fireplaces and wood stoves is difficult to enforce. Therefore, we do not support such a ban. We do focus on good information to limit nuisance.

142633.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of terrace heating, especially not if it runs on non-sustainable energy. At the same time, we see this primarily as the responsibility of residents and entrepreneurs. We do not support a ban, also because enforcement is difficult.

142634.explanation: The CDA does not see organizing a fireworks show as a task for the municipality but prefers to leave this to initiatives from residents and entrepreneurs.

142636.explanation: The CDA supports stricter enforcement of current noise standards but not tightening those standards.

142639.explanation: The municipality currently has funds that make it possible for people on social assistance to have their membership fees paid. The CDA supports a continuation of these schemes.

142640.explanation: The CDA supports the continuation of the current subsidy to the local broadcaster but sees no reason to allocate additional funds for this purpose.

142646.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) can play an important role in promoting safety. Since their deployment in the municipality is currently limited, the CDA advocates for expanding it.

142647.explanation: The CDA wants to survey during the upcoming council term where residents, especially women, feel unsafe. Increased camera surveillance could then be one of the possible measures to improve safety and the feeling of security.

142649.explanation: The municipality does not have the financial means, expertise, or capacity to provide starter loans. However, for new affordable housing construction, we do want to give priority to residents of the municipality or people who have a social or community bond with the place of residence.

142652.explanation: Recreational homes are, in principle, meant for temporary use, not permanent residence. However, in some emergencies, this should be possible. The most important thing remains that the municipality ensures sufficient quality and affordable housing for its residents.

142653.explanation: We find it important to build a substantial number of new homes. Therefore, we support the development of De Kersenweide, but after that, we want no major expansions outside the village boundaries. We want to preserve the small-scale, village-like, and green character of the municipality.

142654.explanation: For those who can work, the priority lies with (re)integration into the labor market, as prescribed in the Participation Act. The assessment of this is the responsibility of the benefit agencies. If it appears that someone cannot work, it is undesirable to force that person into other community work.

142655.explanation: The municipality currently has such a scheme, and the CDA wants to continue it.

142656.explanation: The CDA wants to continue the current scheme within the framework of poverty reduction but does not want to raise the income limit.

142658.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality must ensure sufficient public charging points, as not everyone has the possibility to install a charging station on private property.

142660.explanation: The CDA thinks it is important that there is sufficient choice among different care providers, but not in all cases will a care provider be available based on one’s own religion, culture, or belief.

142665.explanation: We consider the development of a new business park on the south side of the A12, for existing local entrepreneurs, very important. Local entrepreneurs can then relocate to this business park so they can further develop and be retained for the municipality.

142667.explanation: To properly arrange the sheltering of refugees, it is necessary to provide them with a place. The shortage of social rental homes is not solved by giving refugees no priority but by structurally building more social rental homes.

142671.explanation: Our municipality currently has no coffeeshops. The CDA believes this should remain the case.

142672.explanation: Our municipality ranks as one of the worst in the region regarding waiting times for social rental housing. Therefore, the municipality must take strong action to reduce this waiting time. The CDA wants to realize at least 30% social rental homes in every new construction plan.

142673.explanation: For new construction, the CDA’s starting point is that the small-scale, village-like, and green character of the municipality should be preserved as much as possible. High residential towers do not fit here. Therefore, the CDA advocates for a maximum building height of five floors.

142676.explanation: Since the number of residents in Bunnik will increase significantly due to new construction, we find it desirable to provide sufficient parking spaces with new construction. Some of our (future) residents simply depend on the car for work.

142679.explanation: We want a government that is as close as possible to the people and leaders who understand what is happening locally. For now, we believe the best way to achieve this is with an independent municipality of Bunnik.

142622.explanation: The municipality of Bunnik has a tradition of a municipal government with both men and women. The previous period started with a college of three women and one man. D66 wants the best people. The best men and the best women.

142623.explanation: D66 supports a municipal advisory referendum, as already established by initiative of D66 council member Klaas Maijer in the local referendum ordinance.

142627.explanation: D66 wants the government to help get sustainable initiatives off the ground. If a contribution from the municipality is needed to make an initiative feasible, we want the municipality to think along about gathering sufficient budget.

142628.explanation: D66 does not want to continue sectors that add little to nothing to our economy but cause nuisance. D66 therefore wants to stop building more distribution centers.

142630.explanation: This is national policy! D66 wants municipalities to have more control over housing provisions and for employers to be obliged to guarantee good housing, decent wages, and medical care. Housing seasonal workers on their own company grounds can be part of this.

142632.explanation: Residents are free to use their fireplaces when necessary. However, freedom also means taking responsibility for your behavior and, in our view, requires consideration for your neighbors. D66 wants residents to be informed about responsible fireplace usage.

142633.explanation: Although terrace heaters are not good for the environment, we find a municipal ban to go too far. If you yourself are not enthusiastic about something, that does not immediately mean it should be banned. It is the responsibility of entrepreneurs to use terrace heaters as little as possible.

142634.explanation: D66 is in favor of creating new traditions. The municipality can help establish a modern Bunnik New Year’s Eve celebration. We have previously advocated for this in the municipal council. Unfortunately, there was no majority for it.

142636.explanation: We want a good balance between lively atmosphere and healthy noise levels. Festivities contribute, under conditions, to vibrant villages.

142639.explanation: Sports and culture are important to participate fully in society. D66 therefore wants to maintain the municipal sports fund.

142640.explanation: The funding of local broadcasters has been transferred to the national government. Therefore, from 2026 on, it is no longer a municipal task.

142646.explanation: Our municipality currently has far too little capacity to enforce regulations. For a safe living environment, it is important to be able to act against nuisance. Visible neighborhood officers help with this, but nuisance can also be effectively addressed by BOAs.

142647.explanation: In D66’s view, there is no need for structurally more camera surveillance. If needed, for example due to nuisance, (temporary) camera surveillance can already be deployed.

142649.explanation: A starter loan sounds sympathetic but does not solve the problem. It drives up housing prices and does not create more homes. D66 mainly wants to build more homes so that everyone can rent or buy a suitable, affordable home.

142652.explanation: This is not so much a municipal but mainly a national issue. As a nationally rooted party, we therefore refer to our national position: in this housing crisis, it is not the time to harshly enforce rules against people who have a home, driving the housing market to overheat.

142653.explanation: In the current housing crisis, we cannot exclude any solution. D66 is proud that in the past period we made the construction of Kersenweide possible! That new residential area lies outside existing village boundaries. We are not in favor of building new settlements.

142654.explanation: According to D66, most people are looking for a meaningful life and thus want to make a meaningful contribution to society. Also, people who cannot make ends meet without welfare benefits. D66 does believe everyone should be encouraged to contribute to our society.

142655.explanation: Until there is one good (national) scheme, we want to simplify and merge existing schemes where possible. Less distrust and more trust. For example, D66 wants the municipality’s declaration fund to be automatically paid to people known to the social services.

142656.explanation: Thanks to D66, the group of people eligible for income support was enlarged in the past period. Further increases are not meaningful. More important is that we ensure good jobs with good wages. Residents benefit more in the long term from public services.

142658.explanation: D66 is satisfied with the current demand-driven way in which charging points for electric cars in public spaces can be requested. As a result, the number of spots steadily increases, which is necessary for the further transition to sustainable transport.

142660.explanation: The freedom to choose your own care provider is important to D66. This is legally arranged (in the Health Insurance Act and the Youth Act) and is therefore not something the municipal council can decide on.

142665.explanation: D66 supports a new, green business park near the A12. The proposal for this was recently adopted by the municipal council. At the same time, D66 wants to convert other business parks into residential areas (such as the station area in Bunnik and the old Kodak site in Odijk).

142667.explanation: D66 supports the municipality continuing the current policy, in which a portion of the social rental homes becoming available are reserved for people who urgently need housing, such as those with an urgency declaration, specific target groups, and status holders.

142671.explanation: Opening a coffee shop is not a municipal responsibility. However, allowing a coffee shop can help reduce criminal drug-related disturbances on the streets. At this moment, the opening of a coffee shop is not under consideration.

142672.explanation: D66 focuses on the construction of affordable housing. Sustainable new construction should consist of 30% social rental, 50% mid-range rental and affordable owner-occupied homes, and a maximum of 20% expensive rental or ownership.

142673.explanation: D66 wants buildings that fit well within our villages but also make affordable housing possible. Therefore, we believe new residential buildings may be built higher than currently, for example, in the future transformation of the Bunnik station area. But certainly not everywhere.

142676.explanation: D66 encourages walking, cycling, and public transport. In D66's view, there is no need to increase the number of parking spaces in the different villages.

142679.explanation: The municipality exists for its residents. As long as the municipality of Bunnik can effectively carry out its tasks for its residents, there is no reason to merge. However, if it would be better for our residents to do so, we are open to this.

142622.explanation: The VVD finds it important that the right leader is chosen for the task with attention to inclusivity.

142623.explanation: The municipal council is elected as the people's representative and makes decisions for the municipality of Bunnik. The VVD does find it important that residents are involved. They can make use of participation processes and attend information sessions at the town hall.

142627.explanation: In the municipality of Bunnik, the Energy Cooperative Bunnik is active, helping residents with questions and initiatives that contribute to saving and generating energy. The VVD is pleased with this cooperative and encourages collaboration between the municipality, residents, and the cooperative.

142628.explanation: The VVD supports the development of business activities in our municipality and favors a new business park south of the A12. The limited space on the new business park should primarily be reserved for local businesses or businesses with a strong connection to the municipality.

142630.explanation: Employers should be given the opportunity to provide temporary housing for seasonal workers on their own premises or another suitable location. We give entrepreneurs the freedom to make the best choice for this themselves.

142632.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that residents can make their own choices. We find it important that residents consider one another when burning wood and follow advice that contributes to this.

142633.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs may choose whether or not to use heating on their terraces. The VVD does not want a ban on this.

142634.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show costs a lot of money that is not available. If initiatives arise from the community, we think the municipality should engage in discussions about this.

142636.explanation: The VVD finds it important that current laws and regulations for noise standards are followed. There is no need to make these stricter in advance than the rest of the Netherlands.

142639.explanation: We stand for an active and healthy municipality. Sport and exercise enhance our health, bring people together, and increase self-confidence and well-being among young and old. We encourage cooperation between sports clubs, schools, and out-of-school care, supported by neighborhood sports coaches.

142640.explanation: The VVD values independent journalism and is therefore against granting subsidies for it. We find it important that the municipality focuses on accessible municipal services. The municipality communicates in a timely manner, in clear language, and as visually as possible.

142646.explanation: Community officers (BOAs) are visible in the neighborhoods and effective in addressing minor offenses, recovering damages from offenders, with fine revenues being returned to the municipality as much as possible. In asylum seeker reception, the safety of residents and entrepreneurs always comes first!

142647.explanation: We focus on strengthening safety on all fronts: prevention, digital resilience, crisis management, and strict enforcement. Camera surveillance is deployed in high-risk areas to increase the chance of apprehension and enable effective supervision where it contributes to safety.

142649.explanation: The VVD finds it important that starters have a fair chance at housing in our municipality. However, as the municipality of Bunnik is too small, starter loans are not feasible. Previous construction projects have shown this to be unworkable.

142652.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for permanent residence. The VVD wants to build enough houses so there is sufficient supply in the housing market.

142653.explanation: The VVD supports preserving the green public space within the existing village boundaries while maintaining liveability. Therefore, we want to build outside the existing village boundaries. Where possible, homes can be added within the village boundaries.

142654.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to contribute and participate in society.

142655.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in our society. We focus on support that helps people to actively take part in social life again at their own level and pace. Social provisions exist to help residents move forward, as a springboard to work and independence.

142656.explanation: The VVD aligns with the nationally applicable guidelines in this regard.

142658.explanation: To ensure enough parking spaces with charging stations in the future, it is important to invest in charging stations in the municipality of Bunnik.

142660.explanation: Every person has the right to the greatest possible freedom. That means you may live your life as you want. Freedom is indispensable to develop yourself and get the best out of yourself.

142665.explanation: We create room for entrepreneurs and develop good and accessible business parks that can grow with demand. Specifically, we will realize a new business park south of the A12 in Odijk and focus on developing and expanding existing sites.

142667.explanation: The VVD wants priority for local residents and people socially connected to the municipality of Bunnik in the allocation of social rental homes, so that residents from here can continue to live here. We do not want automatic urgency declarations for status holders.

142671.explanation: The VVD is against establishing a coffee shop in the municipality of Bunnik.

142672.explanation: The VVD supports further growth of our villages, but with preservation of village identity. We focus on affordable rental and owner-occupied homes for the middle segment. The housing supply must fit the demand and location in our municipality.

142673.explanation: The way we build matters. Our village character does not suit a skyline with high-rise buildings taller than five residential floors.

142676.explanation: We want realistic parking standards for new construction and amenities that grow along to allow a good quality of life in Bunnik, Odijk, and Werkhoven. We provide sufficient free parking spaces that meet the needs of residents and visitors so that surrounding neighborhoods are not additionally burdened.

142679.explanation: The VVD would like to keep the municipality of Bunnik as it is now. We mainly collaborate with neighboring municipalities in joint arrangements from which we as a municipality can benefit.

statements.title: Bunnik must merge with a neighboring municipality.
In Bunnik the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Bunnik moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.
Bunnik must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: Someone who works for the municipality and ensures everyone follows the rules.

titleParts.text: s (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.
Bunnik must increase camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.text: Mensen die een bijstandsuitkering krijgen, moeten in ruil daarvoor maatsch

titleParts.glossary: Abbreviation for application, usually referring to a program on a smartphone.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bunnik must create more business parks.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The residents of Bunnik must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.
High residential apartment blocks may be built in Bunnik.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een verblijfsvergunning voorrang blijven geven op een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het kwijtschelden van de

titleParts.glossary: Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: At least 30% of the new homes to be built in Bunnik must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Bunnik moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.
